content: If each of the letters of the English alphabet is assigned an odd numerical value beginning with $$\mathrm{A}=1, \mathrm{~B}=3$$ and so on, what will be the total value of the letters of the word RADICAL?
explanation: $$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{A=1, B=3, C=5, D=7, E=9} \\ & \mathrm{F=11, G=13, H=15, I=17, J=19} \\ & \mathrm{~K}=21, \mathrm{~L}=23, \mathrm{M}=25, \mathrm{~N}=27, \mathrm{O}=29 \\ & \mathrm{P=31, Q=33, R=35, S=37, T=39} \\ & \mathrm{U=41, V=43, W=45, X=47, Y=49} \\ & \mathrm{Z=51} \\ & \therefore \text { RADICAL }=35+1+7+17+5+1+23 =89 \\ \end{aligned}$$
